steps.title: Spot the key claim: regrowth means forest cover increases
steps.content: The passage says increased environmental reforms and declining meat prices “triggered a natural regrowth process.” Regrowth in the context of forest data means forest cover areas must be increasing, particularly from 1979 (when deforestation peaked) to 2000.

steps.title: Find the choice showing all forests recovering
steps.content: From 1979 to 2000, every class increased significantly: Class I-IV from ~400 to ~1000 sq km, Class VI from ~275 to ~525, and Class VIII from ~700 to ~975. This universal recovery across all land use capability classes is the evidence of regrowth. Choices A and C reference static values or coincidences rather than growth trends, while B addresses the deforestation period (1960–1979), not the recovery. That matches choice D — D is correct.
